# episteme

> **episteme installs an epistemic posture.** The artifacts are how the posture becomes enforceable. Markdown. Vendor-neutral. The kernel outlives the tooling.

episteme is the posture layer installed beneath whichever memory substrate and whichever runtime the operator uses. A *posture* is how a reasoner holds themselves before a decision: which questions get asked, which unknowns get named, which options are pre-rejected, and which conditions force a pivot. Tools cycle every 18–36 months; the posture does not. The kernel is a small set of markdown files defining the posture's four observable artifacts (reasoning-surface, decision-trace, verification, handoff), named counters to the most dangerous failure modes, and schema for encoding operator preferences. Adapters mount the same kernel files into specific runtimes (Claude Code, Hermes, future).

## Kernel (load first, in priority order)

- [SUMMARY](./kernel/SUMMARY.md): 30-line distillation of the entire kernel
- [CONSTITUTION](./kernel/CONSTITUTION.md): root claim, four principles, nine failure modes (+2 v1.0 RC)
- [REASONING_SURFACE](./kernel/REASONING_SURFACE.md): Knowns/Unknowns/Assumptions/Disconfirmation protocol (scenario-polymorphic blueprints at v1.0 RC)
- [FAILURE_MODES](./kernel/FAILURE_MODES.md): nine failure modes mapped 1:1 to counter artifacts; v1.0 RC adds modes 10 (framework-as-Doxa) and 11 (cascade-theater)
- [DESIGN_V1_0_SEMANTIC_GOVERNANCE](./docs/DESIGN_V1_0_SEMANTIC_GOVERNANCE.md): v1.0 RC spec — three pillars, four named blueprints, ten CPs
- [KERNEL_LIMITS](./kernel/KERNEL_LIMITS.md): when this kernel is the wrong tool; declared structural gaps
- [OPERATOR_PROFILE_SCHEMA](./kernel/OPERATOR_PROFILE_SCHEMA.md): schema for encoding operator preferences
- [REFERENCES](./kernel/REFERENCES.md): attribution for every load-bearing borrowed concept
- [CHANGELOG](./kernel/CHANGELOG.md): versioned kernel history
- [HOOKS_MAP](./kernel/HOOKS_MAP.md): kernel invariants ↔ runtime enforcement

## Delivery framing

- [INSTALL.md](./INSTALL.md): three install paths (marketplace one-liner, full CLI clone, dev `--plugin-dir`).

## Demos (reference deliverables)

- [01_attribution-audit](./demos/01_attribution-audit/): canonical four-artifact shape (reasoning-surface.json → decision-trace.md → verification.md → handoff.md). The kernel applied to itself.
- [02_debug_slow_endpoint](./demos/02_debug_slow_endpoint/): posture applied to a realistic p95 regression (DROP INDEX hidden in a rename migration). Fluent-wrong answer ("add a cache") rejected at the Core Question gate.
- [03_differential](./demos/03_differential/): same prompt, posture off vs. on. The demo that convinces skeptics. [DIFF.md](./demos/03_differential/DIFF.md) shows what the posture changed and which failure modes it caught.

## Install surfaces

- [.claude-plugin/marketplace.json](./.claude-plugin/marketplace.json): Claude Code marketplace manifest. `/plugin marketplace add junjslee/episteme` installs this repo as a plugin.
- [.claude-plugin/plugin.json](./.claude-plugin/plugin.json): plugin manifest; declares discovery paths for agents, skills, hooks.
- [.claude-plugin/README.md](./.claude-plugin/README.md): plugin install + uninstall instructions.
- [hooks/hooks.json](./hooks/hooks.json): portable plugin hooks using `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}`.

## Benchmark (disconfirmable)

- [benchmarks/kernel_v1/](./benchmarks/kernel_v1/): 20-prompt deterministic scorer with pre-declared disconfirmation target and strict scoring mode. Honest partial PASS with integrity caveats published.

## Capture ergonomics

- `episteme capture --input <file>` — drafts a reasoning-surface.json skeleton from unstructured text. Implementation: [src/episteme/capture.py](./src/episteme/capture.py).

## Agent operating contract

- [AGENTS.md](./AGENTS.md): deterministic build/test/sync, kernel invariants, prohibited patterns, delegation conventions

## Human documentation

- [README](./README.md): project overview, quick start, comparisons
- [docs/ARCHITECTURE.md](./docs/ARCHITECTURE.md): v1.0 RC runtime flowchart with node annotations
- [docs/LAYER_MODEL.md](./docs/LAYER_MODEL.md): layer model + tool/adapter matrix + bootstrap policy
- [docs/SUBSTRATE_BRIDGE.md](./docs/SUBSTRATE_BRIDGE.md): pluggable substrate adapter protocol (mem0, memori, noop reference impls).

## Contracts (governance)

- [docs/MEMORY_CONTRACT.md](./docs/MEMORY_CONTRACT.md): memory class schema + conflict semantics
- [docs/EVOLUTION_CONTRACT.md](./docs/EVOLUTION_CONTRACT.md): propose → critique → gate → promote loop for kernel changes
- [docs/ANTHROPIC_MANAGED_AGENTS_BRIDGE.md](./docs/ANTHROPIC_MANAGED_AGENTS_BRIDGE.md): bridging managed runtimes into the memory contract

## Optional

- [docs/OPEN_SOURCE_YOUR_PROFILE.md](./docs/OPEN_SOURCE_YOUR_PROFILE.md): publishing your operator profile
